steps.title: Recognize the equations describe the same line
steps.content: Multiplying $2x + 3y = 7$ by $5$ gives $10x + 15y = 35$, so both equations represent the same line in the $xy$-plane. Any point that satisfies $2x + 3y = 7$ satisfies the system.

steps.title: Test the choices with a Desmos function
steps.content: Define $L(x,y)=2x+3y$. A correct parametrized point must make $L(x,y)$ equal $7$ for every value of $r$. In Desmos, set $r=[0,1]$ and evaluate each answer choice; the correct one returns $[7,7]$.

steps.title: Test the choices
steps.content:
Substitute each point into $2x + 3y$ and require the result to equal $7$ for every $r$.
  • Choice A — $\left(\frac{r}{5}+7,\,-\frac{r}{5}+35\right)$: $2\!\left(\frac{r}{5}+7\right)+3\!\left(-\frac{r}{5}+35\right) = \frac{2r}{5}+14-\frac{3r}{5}+105 = -\frac{r}{5}+119$, not $7$. Eliminate.
  • Choice B — $\left(-\frac{3r}{2}+\frac{7}{2},\,r\right)$: $2\!\left(-\frac{3r}{2}+\frac{7}{2}\right)+3r = -3r+7+3r = 7$ for every $r$. Works.
  • Choice C — $\left(r,\,\frac{2r}{3}+\frac{7}{3}\right)$: $2r+3\!\left(\frac{2r}{3}+\frac{7}{3}\right) = 2r+2r+7 = 4r+7$, not $7$. Eliminate.
  • Choice D — $\left(r,\,-\frac{3r}{2}+\frac{7}{2}\right)$: $2r+3\!\left(-\frac{3r}{2}+\frac{7}{2}\right) = 2r-\frac{9r}{2}+\frac{21}{2} = -\frac{5r}{2}+\frac{21}{2}$, not $7$. Eliminate.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
